source.title: Results of the c-TRAK TN trial: a clinical trial utilising ctDNA mutation tracking to detect molecular residual disease and trigger intervention in patients with moderate- and high-risk early-stage triple-negative breast cancer.

finding.description: Rate of ctDNA detection by 12 months was 27.3%.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA detection rate by 12 months was 27.3%.

source_quote: Rate of ctDNA detection by 12 months was 27.3% (44/161, 95% confidence interval 20.6% to 34.9%).

source.title: Results of the c-TRAK TN trial: a clinical trial utilising ctDNA mutation tracking to detect molecular residual disease and trigger intervention in patients with moderate- and high-risk early-stage triple-negative breast cancer.

finding.description: 72% of patients allocated to intervention had metastases on staging at the time of ctDNA+.
finding.effect_summary: 72% of intervention patients had metastases at ctDNA+ detection.

source_quote: Of patients allocated to intervention, 72% (23/32) had metastases on staging at the time of ctDNA+.

source.title: Results of the c-TRAK TN trial: a clinical trial utilising ctDNA mutation tracking to detect molecular residual disease and trigger intervention in patients with moderate- and high-risk early-stage triple-negative breast cancer.

finding.description: None of the five patients who commenced pembrolizumab achieved sustained ctDNA clearance.
finding.effect_summary: No sustained ctDNA clearance in pembrolizumab-treated patients.

source_quote: Of the five patients who commenced pembrolizumab, none achieved sustained ctDNA clearance.
